w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring NVIDIA A100 GPU and high-tier inference performance for machine learning and AI workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 1 NVIDIA A100 80GB GPU, 12 vCPUs, 170GB RAM, and 375GB local NVMe SSD.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized instance with 1 NVIDIA A100 80GB GPU, 12 vCPUs, 170GB RAM, and 375GB local NVMe SSD, designed for high-performance machine learning workloads.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server built on an x86_64 architecture using Intel Xeon processors. It provides 12 dedicated vCPUs across 6 physical cores with a 2.2 GHz clock speed, supported by 170 GB of RAM. The configuration includes one NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPU with 80 GB of VRAM and 375 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU and memory performance, while single-core tasks show weaker results. However, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference tasks for prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes. The system supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets, alongside essential security features like IBRS and SSBD. Given the specialized GPU hardware and high-tier inference benchmarks, this server is specifically engineered for machine learning, deep learning model training, and high-performance AI inference workloads.
